ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing A Report On The Investment Strategy
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Collier for and with Walter Hansell.

The Court expressed concern in that the investment strategy cost the trust 1/2 of its value, that it is now more diversified but still heavily invested in the same sectors and questions if it is a good strategy. Mr. Hansell stated that it was an over concentrated portfolio which was done for a reason but did not work out, that they were trying to obtain reliable current income, that the things that went awry had substantial impact, that they were badly affected by the continued decline of interest rates, that energy production was to be a reliable income but was driven by commodities that went off the cliff in 2014, that they liquidated as the market declined, that it is now more diversified and currently meant to provide current cash income. The Court stated that it is still heavy in oil and gas. Mr. Hansell stated that the largest investment (TC Pipelines) is not a commodity priced producer. The Court asked about the form of investments. Mr. Hansell reported that portions are with a perspective on evolution of economy such as Google & Apple, that TC Pipelines is a specific stock, it is on the NYSE as a limited partnership and functions as a liquid traded stock and that he has invested in 2 mutual funds. The Court asked about the beneficiaries circumstance. Mr. Hansell gave a background on the beneficiaries disability stating that he has full intellectual capacity, that his parents are his full time caregivers and that he could possibly live a full life. The Court asked if he is satisfied that the current structure is adequate. Mr. Hansell responded yes but that he cannot predict the future. The Court stated that the trustee can be held responsible for not diversifying and not being conservative. Mr. Hansell stated that he has avoided ETF's and investments with fees, that he is reluctant to choose a fully diversified portfolio and that 12% of the portfolio is still in REITS. The Court stated that the trust provides discretion to the trustee, that the aggregate returns are to be reasonable, that it requires special attention, that it is inclined to allow the accounting but set a status for a report on the investment strategy and seeking approval of the strategy with a reason as to why it was chosen and asked how long the new strategy has been in place. Mr. Hansell responded that it has been in place since early 2015 and that the values are up about 25% this year, and that it is still what he believes to be a prudent strategy. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order) and set a status on filing a report on the investment strategy for February 1, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The report is to be filed no later than January 25, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Collier for and with Walter Hansell (Successor Trustee).

Mr. Collier stated that City National insisted on resigning, that Mr. Hansell stepped in and took over, that it was required that all assets be liquidated, that the portfolio was entirely in cash and that it had to be invested. In addition, he stated that Mr. Hansell relied on the provision in the trust that gave him authority to hire and pay the attorney and that it would not happen again. Mr. Hansell stated that he selected investments that he thought were appropriate, spoke of a $70,000.00 capital gain, stated that they were long term investments, that he made adjustments, that he sold those securities that had declined and that the portfolio was up 20%. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
